name: PWA Install & Cache Engine
description: Native installation, multi-strategy caching per resource type, and real connectivity detection: the three pillars that hold up this PWA.
longDescription: Installation via beforeinstallprompt, a Service Worker with three different cache strategies per resource type (Network First for HTML, Cache First for images, Stale-While-Revalidate for JS/CSS, via Serwist), and an online/offline status indicator that does a real ping to /api/health instead of relying solely on navigator.onLine.
tags: PWA, Service Worker, Cache Strategies, Serwist, installable, offline-first
architecture: Offline-first, Service Worker as network proxy, multi-strategy caching via Serwist
technologies: Web App Manifest, Service Worker, Serwist (built on Workbox)
concepts: Progressive enhancement, offline-first, network proxy pattern, cache strategy per asset type
installIntro1: A Progressive Web App (PWA) is an application built with web platform technologies that delivers a user experience similar to a platform-specific native app.
installIntro2: Even though it's built with traditional web technologies like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, a PWA can offer features common to native apps, such as home screen installation, offline functionality, notifications, and integration with native OS APIs.
installIntro3: In practice, the idea behind a PWA is to bring the experience of a traditional website closer to that of a native app you'd install from your smartphone's app store.
installBenefitsTitle: What do you gain by using a PWA?
installBenefit1: Smoother experience: the app can open like a regular app, straight from the device's home screen.
installBenefit2: Better performance: thanks to caching and local storage, many screens can load faster.
installBenefit3: Offline support: depending on the implementation, parts of the app can keep working even without internet.
installBenefit4: One app for multiple platforms: the same codebase can serve desktop, Android, and iPhone.
installLimitsTitle: What does a PWA still lack compared to native apps?
installLimit1: Less access to the OS: some of the device's more advanced features are still limited in the browser.
installLimit2: Cross-browser compatibility: some browsers, especially on iPhone, restrict certain PWA features.
installLimit3: Extreme performance: very heavy applications, like complex games or 3D rendering, still perform better as native apps.
installSummary: In short: a PWA tries to combine the easy distribution of a website with the experience of an installed app.
installHowTo1: To install the app on your device, click the button below. This will open the operating system's native install prompt.
installConditionsIntro: If the button isn't available, check whether you're under any of these conditions below:
installCondition1: Incognito tab: browsers usually block PWA installation in incognito or private tabs.
installCondition2: Service Worker issues: without a valid registered Service Worker, many browsers stop considering the app an installable PWA.
installCondition3: Browser compatibility: some browsers have partial support for PWAs, especially on iPhone.
installCondition4: Lack of user interaction: some browsers only show the install option after the user has navigated or interacted with the site for a while.
installManualIntro: If none of the conditions above apply to you, you can try installing it manually as shown below:
installManualDesktop: Desktop: click the install icon in the browser's address bar
installManualMobile: Mobile: open the browser menu and select "Add to Home Screen"
cacheServiceWorkerDesc: A Service Worker is a script the browser runs in the background. It acts as an intermediary layer between the app and the internet. In practice, the Service Worker can intercept the app's requests, store files locally, and define custom behaviors such as offline functionality, smart caching, and push notifications.
cacheDesc: Cache is a way of temporarily storing files and data on the user's device. Instead of re-downloading images, styles, scripts, or data every time the user opens the app, the browser can reuse files already stored locally. This reduces unnecessary loading and improves the app's speed.
cacheSummary: In PWAs, the Service Worker typically works together with the Cache to make the app faster, more stable, and even partially functional without internet.
cacheStrategyHtml: HTML — Network First (2s timeout): Always tries to fetch the latest version from the network. If the response takes longer than 2 seconds or fails, it falls back to the cache. As a last resort, it redirects to the /offline page.
cacheStrategyImages: Images — Cache First: Returns immediately from the cache without hitting the network, guaranteeing instant loading. Ideal for static assets that rarely change.
cacheStrategyJsCss: JS/CSS — Stale While Revalidate: Returns the cached version immediately and, in parallel, fetches the updated version in the background for the next visit.
statusIntro: Connectivity status is checked in real time via a ping to the /api/health route. The component monitors the browser's online/offline events and shows how long the app has been in each state.
statusDesktopTest1: If you're on Desktop, you can simulate a connection loss by pressing F12 and opening the browser's "Network" tab.
statusDesktopTest2: In the selector that usually shows "No throttling", select the "Offline" option. This will make the browser temporarily block internet access for the page.
statusMobileTest: If you're on a mobile device, just disconnect from Wi-Fi for a few moments to experience the offline behavior.
statusPwaNote: In a PWA, part of the app can keep working thanks to caching and Service Workers.
statusMdnTestBefore: An interesting test is comparing this behavior with traditional sites, like
statusMdnTestAfter: Since the site doesn't work as a fully offline PWA, the browser will likely show its default connection failure screen.
statusResetTest: To go back to normal, just set the option back to "No throttling". In some cases, you may need to refresh the page by pressing F5.
statusAppOnlineMessage: All good! We're connected with you.
statusAppOfflineMessage: We couldn't connect to the server right now
statusAppEstimateNote: This is an estimate based on your device's networking APIs.
